---
title: "Your First Map"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Reproducing the Python geospatial course in R

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(here)


```

```{r}
full_data <- read_sf(here("data/DEC_lands/DEC_lands/DEC_lands.shp"))
```

```{r}
View(full_data)
```

```{r}
data <- select(full_data, c("CLASS", "COUNTY", "geometry"))
```

```{r}
data %>% 
    filter(!is.na(CLASS)) %>% 
    count(CLASS, sort = TRUE)
```
```{r}
wild_lands <- data %>% 
    filter(CLASS %in% c('WILD FOREST', 'WILDERNESS'))
```

```{r}
head(wild_lands)
```
```{r}
# plot(st_geometry(wild_lands))
plot(wild_lands)

```

```{r}

# Campsites in New York state (Point)
POI_data <- read_sf(here("data/DEC_pointsinterest/DEC_pointsinterest/Decptsofinterest.shp"))
campsites <- POI_data %>% 
    filter(ASSET == "PRIMITIVE CAMPSITE")

# Foot trails in New York state (LineString)
roads_trails <- read_sf(here("data/DEC_roadstrails/DEC_roadstrails/Decroadstrails.shp"))
trails <- roads_trails  %>% 
    filter(ASSET == "FOOT TRAIL")

# County boundaries in New York state (Polygon)
counties <- read_sf(here("data/NY_county_boundaries/NY_county_boundaries/NY_county_boundaries.shp"))
```


```{r}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = counties, fill = NA) +
    geom_sf(data = wild_lands, color = 'lightgreen') +
    geom_sf(data = campsites, size = 0.6, color = 'maroon') +
    geom_sf(data = trails, size = 0.3, color = 'black') +
    theme_minimal()
```
